events.label: reasoning
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Anchor bolts field-bent to fit columns — cold bending of installed anchor bolts to correct misalignment is not permitted without engineering approval and typically exceeds AISC 303 erection tolerances.",
    "Bent anchor bolts reduce effective embedment and cross-sectional capacity, creating a defective connection at the column base."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop loading/erecting on affected columns and isolate the area for correction.",
    "Notify the structural engineer of record (EOR) for review and approved repair/replacement procedure.",
    "Replace bent anchor bolts or install engineer-approved remediation (e.g., new anchors, base plate modification) before proceeding."
  ],
  "rationale": "Field-bending anchor bolts to make them fit indicates the bolts were installed out of tolerance and were altered without approved procedure. AISC 303 limits anchor bolt location tolerances and does not allow arbitrary cold bending of anchor bolts to correct misalignment; OSHA 1926 Subpart R requires proper alignment and stability during erection. The condition is non-compliant and structurally defective.",
  "references": ["AISC 303", "OSHA 1926 Subpart R"]
}
